published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 defines the fortified space but gives no general weighted-array decomposition or count.
YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5 states the injectivity premise but does not give a finite-support universal dimension obstruction.
YuShiTchetgen2025 treats gamma as an analyst-chosen lower bound but does not derive the monotone finite-support slack boundary.
YuShiTchetgen2025 Remark 2.3 gives a 12-by-15 illustration; the proposed 12-by-16 correction is a localized audit consequence of the new count.
AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 and Gu2025BlessingDependence establish generic-identifiability methods for different constrained latent-model classes, not this fortified-completeness completion problem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:universal-rank-barrier and prop:slack-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The barrier is conditional on the selected component intersected with full-cell support and concerns only YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: For full-cell laws in the selected valid-subset component, negative slack rules out fortified outcome-completeness injectivity; it makes no claim about Assumption 4, ATE nonidentification, or alternative bridge routes.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 9--12, equation (2.3), Assumptions 4--5, Remark 2.3, and Theorem 2.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper defines H_gamma through conditional-mean-zero restrictions, states Assumption 5 as injectivity of pairing against H_gamma + L2(Z_-nu*,X), gives a 12-by-15 binary-U illustration, and Theorem 2.1 uses Assumptions 3, 4, 5 and additionally Assumption 1 for ATE identification.

source_verification_receipts.locator: publisher abstract and journal metadata; full text is subscriber-restricted
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The journal record states that at least two independent proxy variables satisfying a rank condition permit nonparametric causal-effect identification.

The article studies proximal identification and inference when integral-equation solutions are not unique, while retaining identification and estimation of the relevant mean functional under its conditions.
The paper develops algebraic/Kruskal arguments for generic identifiability in finite latent-structure models, where exceptional nonidentifiable sets are algebraic or measure-zero.
Gu studies a binary-latent star-forest measurement model, gives graphical identifiability criteria, and shows that structural dependence controls a nontrivial generic-identifiability geometry.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119, 2024, pp. 1778--1793
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper reduces user-specified discrete causal bounding problems to polynomial programs and computes sharp or valid anytime bounds; it does not supply the fortified pairing's weighted dimension or rank-feasibility threshold.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Construct one compatible positive-table completion with a nonzero maximal minor, then prove generic row-rank attainment on the relevant irreducible completion component; this would convert the arithmetic necessary frontier into an exact generic feasibility frontier.
Add a short formal correction of YuShiTchetgen2025 Remark 2.3 showing why L2(Z2) contributes three, rather than two, complement directions in the ternary example.

Add a boundary corollary comparing the outcome-completeness barrier with the paper's alternative fortified treatment-bridge route, explicitly delimiting which identification route remains viable below the frontier.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 with the conditional full-cell, Assumption-5-only scope fixed; prioritize the compatible-minor attainment construction as the substantive strengthening path.
